respectively); each contained more than 95% of the
The Ar beam from the Lawrence Berkeley
laboratory SuperHILAC was defined by two four-jaw
collimators (2mmx2mm) and an anti scattering shield.
Beam intensity was measured by a Faraday cup and two
fixed monitor detectors after traversal of targets.
detected with a gas ionization telescope (GT)
operated with methane gas and a polypropylene window
of 35 microg/cm2. The angle of this telescope with
respect to the beam was denoted thetac. The direction
from target to GT and the beam line define the
reaction plane for light particles detected in
coincidence with heavy fragments. The 500 microm Si
stopping detector in the GT was calibrated for energy
and pulse-height detect with fission fragments from
252Cf and the elastically scattered beam. Atomic
numbers (Z) for the fragments were determined
